title: Ukrainian forces report tactical advances in southern Zaporizhzhia sector near Robotyne
summary: OSINT analyst Borrowed7Time reported verified Ukrainian territorial gains of approximately 3km along the southern Zaporizhzhia axis near Robotyne, with Russian forces withdrawing to secondary defensive positions.
contents:
# Ukrainian Forces Advance Near Robotyne in Southern Zaporizhzhia

## Operational Update
Multiple sources confirmed Ukrainian mechanized forces achieved tactical advances along the southern axis near Robotyne in Zaporizhzhia Oblast.

## Verified Developments
- Ukrainian forces pushed approximately 3km south of previous positions
- Russian units withdrew from two forward observation posts
- Mechanized infantry supported by drone-guided artillery struck defensive positions
- Ukrainian engineering units cleared minefields along approach routes

## Tactical Assessment
- Advances threaten Russian supply lines connecting Tokmak to forward positions
- Ukrainian forces now control elevated terrain providing observation advantage
- Russian forces reinforcing secondary defensive belt 5km south
- Weather conditions favored ground operations with firm terrain

## Verification Status
- Geolocated footage confirms positional changes
- Multiple independent OSINT accounts corroborate the advances
- Satellite imagery from commercial providers shows new Ukrainian positions

## Strategic Context
These gains represent the most significant positional changes in the Zaporizhzhia sector since February 2026, suggesting renewed offensive capability after winter regrouping.
